title: Method: pane.resize
text:
Method name: `pane.resize`

Parameters (REQUIRED):

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `selector` | string | REQUIRED | A pane selector string per [[RFC-0001:C-SELECTORS]]. |
| `resize_type` | string | REQUIRED | Either `"increase"` or `"decrease"`. |
| `direction` | string or null | OPTIONAL | One of `"left"`, `"right"`, `"up"`, `"down"`, or `null`. |
| `step` | integer | OPTIONAL | Number of resize steps to apply. Defaults to `1`. MUST be >= 1. |

The plugin MUST resolve the selector to exactly one pane.

If zero panes match, the plugin MUST return `no_match`.

If multiple panes match, the plugin MUST return `ambiguous_match`.

If `resize_type` is not `"increase"` or `"decrease"`, the plugin MUST return `invalid_params`.

If `direction` is provided and is not one of `"left"`, `"right"`, `"up"`, `"down"`, the plugin MUST return `invalid_params`.

If `step` is less than 1, the plugin MUST return `invalid_params`.

When `direction` is `null` or omitted, the plugin MUST construct an undirected `ResizeStrategy` (no direction component), causing Zellij to resize the pane equally in all applicable directions.

When `direction` is provided, the plugin MUST construct a directed `ResizeStrategy` with the specified direction.

The plugin MUST set `invert_on_boundaries` to `true` on the `ResizeStrategy`.

The plugin MUST call `resize_pane_with_id` once per step.

On success, the response MUST contain `{"resized": "<pane_id>"}`.

Rationale: Step-based relative resizing is the primitive exposed by Zellij's plugin API — absolute sizing (resize to N columns) is implemented client-side by the CLI (per the `resize_to` function) as a polling loop over this method. The `invert_on_boundaries` flag ensures that panes at the edge of the layout still resize correctly by pushing the opposite border. Direction is optional because undirected resize is useful when the caller does not know the pane's position in the layout.
